name: influence-psychology-of-persuasion
description: 10 skills for ethical persuasion strategy, influence defense, and content optimization, extracted from Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion by Robert B. Cialdini

skills.name: influence-principle-selector
skills.description: Identify which of Cialdini's 6 influence principles to apply for a persuasion scenario — scoring each principle against the situation, flagging ethical boundaries, and producing a rationale-backed recommendation.

skills.name: persuasion-content-auditor
skills.description: Audit any persuasive content against the 6 principles of influence and produce a scored report with per-principle ratings, evidence citations, and specific rewrite recommendations.

skills.name: reciprocity-strategy-designer
skills.description: Design reciprocity-based persuasion strategies and detect when reciprocity is being used against you — covering give-first campaigns, rejection-then-retreat sequences, and concession-based selling.

skills.name: commitment-escalation-architect
skills.description: Design commitment escalation sequences and detect consistency pressure — covering onboarding flows, foot-in-the-door campaigns, progressive commitment ladders, and defense against manufactured consistency traps.

skills.name: social-proof-optimizer
skills.description: Optimize social proof strategy using uncertainty and similarity conditions — covering testimonials, user counts, trust badges, FOMO messaging, and defense against manipulated social evidence.

skills.name: liking-factor-engineer
skills.description: Analyze and engineer liking to increase rapport, persuasion, and compliance in marketing, sales, and communication contexts — covering similarity, familiarity, association, and defense against liking exploitation.

skills.name: authority-signal-designer
skills.description: Design and audit authority signals in content, credentials, bios, and landing pages — covering the three authority symbol types, strategic self-deprecation, and the two-question defense framework.

skills.name: scarcity-framing-strategist
skills.description: Design and evaluate scarcity framing for offers, products, and campaigns using psychological research on loss aversion and reactance — covering time limits, quantity limits, and defense against fake scarcity.

skills.name: influence-defense-analyzer
skills.description: Detect and counter manipulation attempts using Cialdini's 6 influence principles — classifying each tactic as ethical or exploitative and prescribing a principle-specific response strategy.

skills.name: multi-principle-stacking-planner
skills.description: Design a layered persuasion campaign by combining 2–4 Cialdini influence principles in the right sequence — covering stacking patterns, principle interaction rules, contrast amplification, and ethical stacking thresholds.

edges.reason: Defense analysis requires knowing which principle is being activated and its ethical classification
Stacking planner needs principle scores and rationale before designing the combination sequence
Stacking planner references reciprocity implementation rules for sequencing decisions
Stacking planner references commitment escalation patterns for sequencing decisions
Stacking planner references social proof conditions for sequencing decisions
Stacking planner references liking factors for sequencing decisions
Stacking planner references authority signal types for sequencing decisions
Stacking planner references scarcity framing rules for sequencing decisions

minimal.description: Entry point — principle selector and content auditor for quick persuasion analysis

core.description: All 6 principle skills plus selector and auditor — full coverage for designing and evaluating persuasion

full.description: Complete persuasion toolkit including defense analysis and multi-principle stacking
